headline: Agent infrastructure and model releases dominated the month as Anthropic shipped Sonnet 5 and a science research app while evaluation and trajectory tooling broke out.
body: A cluster of technique concepts broke out together this month, led by trajectory data, agent skills, behavioral auditing, vulnerability detection, ablation, and semantic chunking, alongside new entrants Gradient Staleness and masking. On the product side Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5, which closes the benchmark gap with Opus, and shipped Claude Science in public beta for reproducible scientific workflows. Tooling for agents advanced in parallel, with Google Cloud detailing continuous evaluation pipelines for multi-agent systems using shadow deployments and tool-use quality metrics, and OpenAI's Greg Brockman framing persistent agents and compute scaling as the path to personal AGI. Cost economics also surfaced, with analysis of tokenmaxxing and test-time compute driving rising AI prices.
